project_goals: RoboDeploy: a maintainable robotics framework with clear layers (backend, task, policy, sensors) and registry-based discovery.
Support multiple backends (MuJoCo, Isaac Sim, ROS2 real/RViz, Gazebo) with minimal robot-specific code in the core library.
Enable N robots : K tasks : M policies with arbitration, selectors, and a robot-centric aggregate model.
Sim-to-real alignment: shared contracts (Observation, Action, SceneSpec), presets for ROS2 control families, and safe hardware pacing.
Keep user/example code free of ROS2 boilerplate while centralizing transport, devtools, and visualization inside robodeploy.

architectural_decisions.decision: One axis of variation per layer (IBackend, ITask, IPolicy, ISensor) with explicit data contracts in core/types.
architectural_decisions.reason: Enforce modularity and testability instead of monolithic env classes.
architectural_decisions.goal: Make swapping sim, real hardware, or policies a local change without ripple refactors.

architectural_decisions.decision: Registry pattern (@register_*) plus use() for optional string-based wiring alongside direct injection.
architectural_decisions.reason: Balance config-driven demos with the preferred pattern of passing concrete classes.
architectural_decisions.goal: Support plugins and user packages without hardcoding imports in RoboEnv.

architectural_decisions.decision: Robot-centric model: Robot + RobotTask replace global RobotConfig/TaskConfig lists on RoboEnv.
architectural_decisions.reason: Per-robot tasks, policies, and arbitration belong with the robot aggregate.
architectural_decisions.goal: Scale cleanly to multi-robot with per-robot task_weights, ITaskSelector, and IPolicySelector.

architectural_decisions.decision: IBackend.initialize(description, scene: SceneSpec, sensors) instead of passing ITask.
architectural_decisions.reason: Scene composition can come from multiple tasks; backend should not depend on a single task object.
architectural_decisions.goal: Correct multi-task / merged scene initialization for initialize_multi.

architectural_decisions.decision: Split ROS2 into ROS2RealBackend (transport, is_real=True) and ROS2GazeboBackend (sim via ROS, is_real=False).
architectural_decisions.reason: Gazebo lifecycle and real hardware differ; one class mixed both concerns.
architectural_decisions.goal: Clear semantics for sim vs real and independent launcher behavior (e.g., RSP with Gazebo).

architectural_decisions.decision: Ros2Runtime singleton: single rclpy.init and shared MultiThreadedExecutor across components.
architectural_decisions.reason: Multiple nodes calling init caused RuntimeError and flaky startup.
architectural_decisions.goal: Deterministic ROS2 process lifecycle for backends, sensors, and devtools.

architectural_decisions.decision: Stable robodeploy/ros2 package: runtime shim, Ros2NodeAdapter, resolve_ros_topic for controllers and sensors.
architectural_decisions.reason: ROS2 should be a reusable transport layer, not scattered rclpy in each module.
architectural_decisions.goal: One place for executor/node lifecycle and namespaced topic construction.

architectural_decisions.decision: RvizPublisher moved to robodeploy/viz/ with backward-compatible shim under backends/real/ros2/rviz.py.
architectural_decisions.reason: Visualization is not backend-specific; MuJoCo/Isaac bridges also publish to RViz.
architectural_decisions.goal: Neutral viz module usable from any backend without importing real-backend internals.

architectural_decisions.decision: ActionTrajectory seqlock ring buffer + ControlLoop as multiprocessing.Process in RoboBridge.
architectural_decisions.reason: Real-time control needs GIL escape and non-blocking handoff from inference.
architectural_decisions.goal: Safer timing and isolation between control and policy threads/processes.

architectural_decisions.decision: ROS2 presets (e.g. kuka_jtc) as data-only config on RobotDescription.ros2_preset_name.
architectural_decisions.reason: Avoid hardcoding controller topics per robot class in backend code.
architectural_decisions.goal: Users pick a preset string; backend resolves joint command and trajectory topics consistently.

architectural_decisions.decision: RobotStatePublisherLauncher uses temp YAML params file and remaps /joint_states to /<robot_id>/joint_states.
architectural_decisions.reason: CLI length limits and default RSP subscription to global /joint_states broke multi-robot RViz.
architectural_decisions.goal: Reliable robot_description + TF per namespace for RViz RobotModel.

architectural_decisions.decision: Fake joint-position sim lives under robodeploy/ros2/devtools/; examples only thin-import wrappers.
architectural_decisions.reason: User asked to remove rclpy/Ros2Runtime from examples while keeping an example entrypoint.
architectural_decisions.goal: Robot-agnostic devtool config (robot_ns, joint_names, topics) with no KUKA logic inside robodeploy/ros2/*.

architectural_decisions.decision: EnvRouter encapsulates normalize_explicit_actions and task/robot action resolution from RoboEnv.
architectural_decisions.reason: RoboEnv grew too many routing helpers; SRP suggests a dedicated orchestration helper.
architectural_decisions.goal: Smaller env.py and easier unit testing of routing rules.

architectural_decisions.decision: GazeboLauncher decomposed into RosGzBridgeLauncher, UrdfSpawner, ControllerSpawner orchestrated by GazeboLauncher.
architectural_decisions.reason: Single file was hard to test and extend for different spawn/bridge strategies.
architectural_decisions.goal: Composable sim launch with clearer failure points and reuse.

architectural_decisions.decision: IRos2Sensor + registry + LastValueCache; first concrete Ros2RgbdCameraSensor.
architectural_decisions.reason: Sensors need the same SOLID boundaries as policies and backends.
architectural_decisions.goal: Pluggable ROS2 sensor streams without coupling Observation construction to one backend.

architectural_decisions.decision: MuJoCo actuator resolution uses robot_id prefix with allow_actuator_name_fallback opt-in.
architectural_decisions.reason: Hardcoded robot0/actuators hid misconfiguration and broke multi-robot naming.
architectural_decisions.goal: Fail-fast by default; explicit escape hatch for legacy MJCF naming.

architectural_decisions.decision: Sensor pair resolution is backend-aware, not just sim-vs-real. Canonical sensor names now support per-backend overrides, so ROS2-backed envs resolve ROS sensor adapters while unsupported backends fail explicitly.
architectural_decisions.reason: Using only `is_real` incorrectly mapped `wrist_camera` and similar canonical names to MuJoCo sensor classes for Gazebo and ROS2/RViz backends.
architectural_decisions.goal: Keep string-based sensor construction aligned with the active backend without breaking direct sensor injection or legacy sim/real fallbacks.

architectural_decisions.decision: ROS2GazeboBackend derives camera bridge topics from declared robot/shared sensors and synthesizes a temporary SDF world from `SceneSpec` only when `config.sim.world` is omitted.
architectural_decisions.reason: Gazebo parity was depending on legacy `robotX.sensors` config fragments and required a hand-authored world file even when tasks already declared the environment through `SceneSpec`.
architectural_decisions.goal: Keep explicit Gazebo worlds authoritative while letting the shared scene contract and declared sensor wiring drive the default launch path.

architectural_decisions.decision: Isaac Sim now participates in backend-aware canonical sensor resolution for wrist/overhead cameras and wrist FT, while its USD scene loader preserves prop/camera orientation in stage state and editable prop-pose updates.
architectural_decisions.reason: Isaac scene loading had partial world support but still lost orientation data, and canonical sensor construction failed for Isaac-specific camera/FT requests despite the backend already merging sensor data.
architectural_decisions.goal: Bring Isaac closer to the shared MuJoCo/Gazebo sensor-and-scene contract without requiring Isaac Sim to be installed just to test the contract wiring.

architectural_decisions.decision: TaskBase now carries a privately bound backend reference so object-centric tasks can read live prop poses through backend scene-edit capabilities without changing the public `ITask.reward_fn()` / `success_fn()` signatures.
architectural_decisions.reason: Manipulation tasks needed object-aware rewards and success checks, but the existing task interface only passed observations and actions during evaluation.
architectural_decisions.goal: Preserve the current task contract while letting tasks become object-aware whenever the active backend can expose prop state.

architectural_decisions.decision: Advanced policy runtime hooks are now consumed directly by the runtime: shared single-policy tasks batch via `get_action_batch()`, policy arbitration notifies rejected candidates, and RoboBridge honors slower policy-side `action_hz` values while replaying the latest action at backend control rate.
architectural_decisions.reason: The advanced hooks already existed on `IPolicy`/`PolicyBase`, but the runtime still treated every policy like a simple per-step single-action function.
architectural_decisions.goal: Normalize the runtime around the existing policy contract before building VLA and diffusion implementations on top of it.

architectural_decisions.decision: Task-scoped language now rides alongside the policy-facing observation, and the VLA slot is a concrete multimodal policy that either calls injected predictors on a real packet or falls back to a lightweight image+instruction heuristic instead of remaining a stub.
architectural_decisions.reason: Advanced policies needed a clean multimodal contract without VLA-only runtime hacks, and the repo still shipped only a placeholder VLA class.
architectural_decisions.goal: Make multimodal observation + language available through the existing env/task/policy contract while landing an immediately usable VLA implementation.

architectural_decisions.decision: DiffusionPolicy now uses the normalized runtime as a real sequence policy: it emits action plans, replans after rejected actions, and supports optional single/batch plan predictors instead of remaining a one-step placeholder.
architectural_decisions.reason: The runtime had already been normalized for advanced hooks, but diffusion remained a stub with no action-sequence semantics.
architectural_decisions.goal: Land a concrete diffusion-style policy that can benefit from the existing batching/rejection/runtime contract without forcing heavyweight dependencies.

architectural_decisions.decision: SensorRig is the user-facing perception composition layer; policies and tasks consume Observation.objects/images/ft_forces instead of backend.get_prop_pose().
architectural_decisions.reason: Sensors were attached as ad-hoc lists without a rig abstraction or a perception-first contract for policies/tasks.
architectural_decisions.goal: Declare logical sensors once (wrist_rgbd, wrist_ft, prop_pose) and resolve sim/real/Gazebo implementations through the registry.

architectural_decisions.decision: RoboEnv.from_config is the canonical preset path; sensor_rigs, obs_pipeline transforms, and PolicyBase.bind_runtime wire MuJoCo IK without example-only hooks.
architectural_decisions.reason: CLI and env_from_preset used RoboEnv.make() which dropped sensor_rigs; reach policies required manual attach_mujoco after reset.
architectural_decisions.goal: Reproducible YAML presets and automatic runtime binding for working pick-place demos.

architectural_decisions.decision: Gazebo URDF sensor injection patches robot descriptions from SensorMount before spawn; ros_gz_bridge derives image and wrench rules from declared sensors.
architectural_decisions.reason: Gazebo launched worlds from SceneSpec but robot-mounted cameras/FT sensors were not inserted into URDF or bridged to ROS topics.
architectural_decisions.goal: Same SensorRig logical names work on MuJoCo, ROS2/RViz, and Gazebo with backend-specific topic defaults.

subtask_history.title: Backend-aware sensor resolution and shared env/sensor contract cleanup

implemented: Added per-backend sensor overrides to the shared sensor-pair registry.
Taught `RoboEnv.make()` and `RoboEnv.from_config()` to pass backend-aware sensor resolution context.
Added `sensor_backend_name` metadata to MuJoCo, Isaac Sim, ROS2 real, ROS2 RViz, and Gazebo backends.
Mapped canonical `wrist_camera` to the ROS2 RGBD ISensor adapter for ROS2-backed backends while preserving MuJoCo and direct real fallbacks.
Made unsupported canonical camera requests on Isaac Sim fail clearly instead of silently selecting the MuJoCo renderer.
known_gaps: Isaac Sim still has no canonical built-in camera pair for backend-aware string construction.
Gazebo scene ingestion and mounted-sensor parity are still pending the next subtask.

verification: conda run -n ros2_env python -m compileall on the touched registry/env/backend/sensor/test files
conda run -n ros2_env python -m unittest discover -s tests -> 14 tests OK
git diff --check -> no diff formatting errors, only existing CRLF warnings
ReadLints -> no new actionable issues; one existing optional-import warning remains in MuJoCo backend

next_subtask.title: Gazebo scene ingestion and mounted sensor parity
instructions: Use the new backend-aware registry path instead of adding Gazebo-specific string hacks in env construction.
Focus on getting declared world assets and camera bridge rules into the Gazebo launch/config path first.
Keep MuJoCo as the contract reference and avoid regressing existing ROS2 transport behavior.

subtask_history.title: Gazebo scene ingestion and mounted sensor parity

implemented: ROS2GazeboBackend now derives image bridge rules and readiness topics from declared robot sensors and shared sensors, preserving per-robot ROS namespaces.
Gazebo launch config now auto-fills `robot_urdf` from the robot description when the sim config omits it.
Added a Gazebo SDF scene builder that converts merged `SceneSpec.to_world()` data into a temporary world file when `config.sim.world` is not provided.
Temporary generated Gazebo world files are cleaned up when the backend closes.
known_gaps: If the caller provides an explicit `config.sim.world`, Gazebo still trusts that file instead of merging additional `SceneSpec` props into it.
Generated Gazebo worlds currently cover world props, lights, terrain, and cameras, but do not yet attach scene props to robot links.

verification: conda run -n ros2_env python -m compileall on the touched Gazebo backend, scene builder, and env/sensor capability test files
conda run -n ros2_env python -m unittest discover -s tests -p test_sensor_env_capabilities.py -> 7 tests OK
conda run -n ros2_env python -m unittest discover -s tests -> 16 tests OK
git diff --check -> only existing CRLF conversion warnings, no diff formatting errors
ReadLints on touched Gazebo/test files -> no errors

next_subtask.title: Isaac Sim scene and sensor parity
instructions: Mirror the backend-aware sensor-resolution contract instead of adding backend-specific env hacks.
Use the Gazebo and MuJoCo work as the reference for world ingestion, declared cameras, and mounted-sensor semantics.
Prefer small contract-preserving runtime changes plus focused capability tests before touching advanced policy work.

subtask_history.title: Isaac Sim scene and sensor parity

implemented: Isaac USD scene loading now applies orientation to props and declared cameras instead of dropping rotation on stage writes.
Editable Isaac prop-pose writes now propagate both translation and orientation into USD stage state.
Canonical `wrist_camera` and `overhead_camera` now resolve to Isaac Sim camera sensors through the backend-aware registry path.
Canonical `wrist_ft` now resolves to an Isaac Sim FT sensor that reads measured joint-force hooks when available.
Added mocked tests for Isaac stage loading, camera reads, FT reads, and backend-aware sensor construction.
known_gaps: Actual Isaac Sim runtime was not available on this machine, so verification covered contract wiring and mocked stage/sensor APIs rather than a live Kit session.
The Isaac camera and FT wrappers are best-effort around optional Isaac APIs and may still need small runtime adjustments on a fully provisioned Isaac install.

verification: conda run -n ros2_env python -m compileall on the touched Isaac backend/sensor/test files
conda run -n ros2_env python -m unittest discover -s tests -p test_registry_honesty.py -> 4 tests OK
conda run -n ros2_env python -m unittest discover -s tests -p test_sensor_env_capabilities.py -> 10 tests OK
conda run -n ros2_env python -m unittest discover -s tests -> 19 tests OK

ReadLints on touched Isaac/test files -> no errors

next_subtask.title: Object-aware task semantics and env/sensor verification cleanup
instructions: Shift manipulation tasks away from pure end-effector waypoint rewards and toward object-aware success/reward semantics now that scene props exist across multiple backends.
Keep the task contract aligned with the current backend capabilities, and extend tests only where they materially reduce regression risk.
Use the leader/follower split only for clearly separable task-logic versus verification-cleanup slices.

subtask_history.title: Object-aware task semantics and env/sensor verification cleanup

implemented: TaskBase now binds the active backend privately and exposes helpers for scene prop lookup and live prop pose queries.
RoboEnv now binds tasks to the backend during component initialization so reward/success logic can use runtime prop state.
PickPlaceTask, PourTask, and PegTask now prefer live object poses for reward and success evaluation instead of only measuring end-effector distance to a fixed waypoint.
Added focused unit tests for object-aware pick-place, pour, and peg-insertion task semantics.
known_gaps: Object-aware task evaluation still depends on backend scene-edit support; transport-only backends keep the existing end-effector fallback path.
The new pour semantics are still object-proxy based because the repo has no liquid state or fluid transfer model yet.

verification: conda run -n ros2_env python -m compileall on the touched task/env/test files
conda run -n ros2_env python -m unittest discover -s tests -p test_task_object_semantics.py -> 3 tests OK
conda run -n ros2_env python -m unittest discover -s tests -> 22 tests OK
ReadLints on touched task/env/test files -> no errors

next_subtask.title: Advanced policy runtime contract normalization
instructions: Normalize the runtime around the actual advanced-policy hooks that already exist on `IPolicy`/`PolicyBase` instead of adding new ad hoc policy entry points.
Focus first on runtime consumption of existing batch/rejection/action-rate hooks before adding new policy implementations.
Keep verification tight and prefer focused contract tests over broad speculative coverage.

subtask_history.title: Advanced policy runtime contract normalization

implemented: RoboEnv now batches shared single-policy task inference across robots via `get_action_batch()` when the active task layout allows it.
RobotTask policy arbitration now calls `notify_rejected()` on non-selected policy candidates.
RoboBridge now derives its inference cadence from active policy `action_hz` values when they are slower than backend control rate.
Added focused tests covering shared-policy batching, rejected-policy notification, and bridge-side policy-rate handling.
known_gaps: Batch inference currently targets the honest low-risk case: shared tasks with exactly one policy and no selector. More complex selector/resolver cases still run per-policy/per-task.
Concurrent-task action resolvers still do not emit a rejected-action callback because they may blend rather than reject candidate actions.

verification: conda run -n ros2_env python -m py_compile on the touched policy-runtime files and tests
conda run -n ros2_env python -m unittest discover -s tests -p test_env_refactor.py -> 5 tests OK
conda run -n ros2_env python -m unittest discover -s tests -> 25 tests OK
ReadLints on touched runtime/test files -> no errors

next_subtask.title: Multimodal/language observation path plus VLA implementation
instructions: Build on the normalized policy runtime instead of introducing VLA-specific execution shortcuts.
Connect language instruction and image-bearing observations through the existing task/policy/env contracts first, then land a concrete VLA policy implementation.
Keep tests focused on the new multimodal contract and VLA behavior rather than replaying all existing policy coverage.

subtask_history.title: Multimodal/language observation path plus VLA implementation

implemented: Observation now includes an optional `language_instruction` field for task-scoped policy inputs.
RobotTask now stamps task language into the observation each policy sees, including warmup, arbitration, and batched shared-policy execution.

Replaced the VLA stub with a concrete `VLAPolicy` that builds multimodal packets, supports injected single/batch predictor callables, and falls back to a lightweight image+instruction heuristic when no external model runtime is configured.
Added focused VLA tests covering language injection, packet construction, batch prediction, and heuristic fallback behavior.
known_gaps: The shipped VLA is intentionally lightweight and dependency-free; heavyweight pretrained VLA backends still need explicit integration through injected callables or a future backend-specific adapter.

Language is attached at the task-policy boundary rather than globally to every raw backend observation, which keeps the contract honest for multi-task robots but means generic observation logs only include language after task annotation.

verification: conda run -n ros2_env python -m py_compile on the touched multimodal/VLA files and tests
conda run -n ros2_env python -m unittest discover -s tests -p test_vla_policy.py -> 4 tests OK
conda run -n ros2_env python -m unittest discover -s tests -> 29 tests OK
ReadLints on touched multimodal/VLA files -> no errors

next_subtask.title: DiffusionPolicy on the normalized runtime
instructions: Mirror the VLA approach: fit the current policy/runtime contract first, and keep heavyweight inference optional or injectable instead of pretending unavailable dependencies exist.
Prefer action-sequence aware behavior that can actually benefit from batch/runtime hooks already normalized in goal2-subtask1.
Keep tests focused on concrete diffusion-policy contract behavior and rollout semantics.

subtask_history.title: DiffusionPolicy on the normalized runtime

implemented: Replaced the diffusion stub with a concrete `DiffusionPolicy` that manages multi-step action plans on top of the normalized policy runtime.
DiffusionPolicy now supports injected single-plan and batch-plan predictors while keeping a lightweight fallback sequence planner for dependency-free operation.
Rejected-action notifications now trigger diffusion replanning instead of leaving stale plans in flight.
Added focused diffusion-policy tests covering plan rollout, rejected-action replanning, batch plan prediction, and fallback directional planning.
known_gaps: The shipped diffusion policy is still lightweight and injection-friendly rather than a fully integrated heavyweight diffusion-model runtime.

Batch plan execution currently returns the first action from each predicted plan; persistent per-stream plan queues for shared-policy multi-robot execution would need additional keyed state if that becomes important.

verification: conda run -n ros2_env python -m py_compile on the touched diffusion files and tests

conda run -n ros2_env python -m unittest discover -s tests -> 33 tests OK
ReadLints on touched diffusion files -> no errors

next_subtask.title: Remote advanced policy serving, composition, and final policy drift audit
instructions: Harden remote-serving/composition around the concrete VLA and diffusion contracts already landed instead of inventing a separate serving-only API.
Audit policy/runtime drift at the end so remote composition, batching, rejection hooks, and task/language wiring still agree.
Prefer small honest serving seams with focused tests over speculative infrastructure.

subtask_history.title: Remote advanced policy serving, composition, and final policy drift audit

implemented: Removed the conflicting top-level `robodeploy/policies/remote.py` module; HTTP JSON remote predictors now live in `robodeploy/policies/remote/http_client.py` as `HttpRemotePolicyClient` alongside the existing ZMQ/gRPC `RemotePolicy` package.
VLAPolicy and DiffusionPolicy accept optional `remote_url` / `remote_transport` config to route predictor calls through the HTTP client without a parallel policy API.
Exported `HttpRemotePolicyClient` and `to_jsonable` from `robodeploy.policies.remote` for honest discovery next to `RemotePolicy` and `PolicyServer`.
Added focused remote-client tests for JSON serialization and VLA/diffusion remote transport wiring.
known_gaps: HTTP remote serving is intentionally minimal (JSON POST); production serving should prefer the existing ZMQ/gRPC `PolicyServer` transport where latency matters.
No end-to-end live PolicyServer + VLAPolicy integration test on this machine; coverage is contract/mocked transport only.

verification: conda run -n ros2_env python -m py_compile on remote http_client, VLA, diffusion, and remote policy tests
conda run -n ros2_env python -m unittest discover -s tests -> 36 tests OK
Resolved import collision: `robodeploy.policies.remote` package no longer shadowed by a sibling `remote.py` module

next_subtask.title: Observation pipeline sync and multi-camera schema

subtask_history.title: Observation pipeline sync and multi-camera schema

implemented: ObsPipeline now mirrors named `images`/`depths` into legacy `rgb`/`depth` via `with_primary_fields`.
TIME_WINDOW sync reuses the last processed observation when timestamps fall outside the window; `reset_sync()` clears buffers on env reset.
Backend sensor merge already populated multi-camera dicts; pipeline completes the policy-facing contract.
known_gaps: Per-sensor async ingestion with independent hardware clocks still relies on backend merge timestamps rather than a full multi-buffer synchronizer.

verification: conda run -n ros2_env python -m unittest discover -s tests -p test_obs_pipeline.py -> 2 tests OK
